items.title: Duel tussen Cambuur en FC Eindhoven afgelast
items.summary: De wedstrijd tussen SC Cambuur en FC Eindhoven van vrijdagavond gaat niet door. De twee ploegen zouden tegenover elkaar staan in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie, maar het veld is onbespeelbaar verklaard.

items.title: Bosz looft Flamingo na sterke invalbeurten: 'Heeft juiste mindset te pakken'
items.summary: Peter Bosz erkende op de persconferentie richting het thuisduel met NAC Breda dat Ryan Flamingo het lastig heeft met zijn reserverol, maar bespeurt de laatste weken wel een positieve ontwikkeling bij de 23-jarige verdediger. Flamingo heeft volgens de trainer van PSV de juiste mindset weer te pakken en liet dat ook zien met goede invalbeurten tegen Fortuna Sittard en Newcastle United.

items.title: Spillane's Tower fancied to see off Grey Dawning on Cheltenham Trials Day
items.summary: The Weekend Winners team are back, looking ahead to Cheltenham Trials Day as well as the Great Yorkshire Chase from Doncaster.

items.title: Van Dijk neemt het op voor Slot na 'respectloze' vraag
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk heeft het na het Champions League-duel met Olympique Marseille opgenomen voor Liverpool-trainer Arne Slot. De Oranje-international hekelde de vraagstelling op de persconferentie in aanloop naar dat duel.

items.title: FC Utrecht heeft Karlsson binnen: 'Ik hou van de Eredivisie'
items.summary: FC Utrecht heeft de komst van Jesper Karlsson wereldkundig gemaakt. De 27-jarige aanvaller wordt voor de rest van het seizoen gehuurd van Bologna.

items.title: Spurs in talks for Robertson, Emery shrugs off Tielemans row, and more: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ The latest football news heading into the weekend ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail Dominic This weekend’s Premier League games are the reverse fixtures of the season openers, back in sun-soaked August. Looking back, the most jarring result was Nottingham Forest 3-1 Brentford, with the teams taking very different trajectories since then. “It was certainly a tough day for us all, but it is pretty obvious to see the development that we have made in the months since that, and I am delighted with the progress we have made,” Brentford manager Keith Andrews said yesterday. Continue reading...

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: Fans’ anger after Braga flop shows Forest honeymoon is over for Dyche
items.summary: Initial optimism has faded after arrival of former Burnley manager, who needs to get more from a struggling squad There was plenty for Sean Dyche to stew on as he marched off the pitch at Estádio Municipal de Braga with the frustrations of the Nottingham Forest supporters ringing in his ears. The boos were not universal, but fans who follow the team over land and sea are usually a decent barometer of the prevailing feeling. By the end of a sodden, joyless night in northern Portugal, it felt like false advertising, possibly one for trading standards. It wasn’t supposed to be like this? Dyche cannot be blamed for the dismal conditions but he and the players can be for a listless performance. Beyond a likely Europa League playoff, which will mean another away day to squeeze into the schedule, fans are seemingly concerned where this is all going. Forest were devoid of sharp edges and not only in attack where the absence of a bona fide striker was painfully apparent. It was a miserable night encapsulated by second-half events: Morgan Gibbs-White’s penalty miss, a Ryan Yates own goal 54 seconds later, Dan Ndoye being booked for simulation and Elliot Anderson being sent off. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings: a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.title: Sources: L'pool, Robertson in talks with Spurs
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have entered talks with Liverpool over a potential deal for defender Andy Robertson, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Rodman contract saga ends with record Spirit deal
items.summary: United States forward Trinity Rodman signed a record three-year contract with the Washington Spirit on Thursday to remain at the club, ending months-long uncertainty over the star's future.

items.title: Slot: Liverpool unlikely to make any signings in Jan
items.summary: Arne Slot has admitted he doesn't expect Liverpool to make any signings in the January transfer window but said the club will always look for opportunities in the market.

items.title: Casemiro to leave Man United at end of the season
items.summary: Casemiro will leave Manchester United when his contract expires at the end of the season, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Spurs open talks to sign Liverpool's Robertson
items.summary: Tottenham enter into talks with Liverpool as they look to secure a deal for left-back Andy Robertson.

items.title: Chelsea’s Alyssa Thompson: ‘I think of this as like going away for college like all my friends did’
items.summary: Blues’ record signing on adapting to English football culture, the challenge of leaving family behind and being well taught by Sonia Bompastor Alyssa Thompson is no stranger to the limelight. Despite being only 21, her undeniable natural talent and eye‑catching career have propelled her into the headlines ever since her hometown club, Angel City, made her the first pick in the 2023 National Women’s Soccer League (NWSL) draft. The past few months, however, have provided the USA international with a different challenge altogether. A high-profile deadline day move to the English champions, Chelsea, in September meant leaving her family and the comforts of Los Angeles and testing herself overseas. Continue reading...

items.title: Uncapped trio named in England Six Nations squad
items.summary: Steve Borthwick has named three uncapped players in Greg Fisilau, Vilikesa Sela and Emmanuel Iyogun as part of his 36-player England Six Nations squad.

items.title: Celtic pick up where they left off under O'Neill - Trusty
items.summary: Celtic defender Auston Trusty praises the impact of Martin O'Neill and his coaching staff after their 2-2 Europa League draw in Bologna.

items.title: Wolves reject £33m Leeds bid for Strand Larsen
items.summary: Leeds United fail with a bid to sign Wolves striker Jorgen Strand Larsen.

items.title: Rodman 'becomes highest-paid women's footballer'
items.summary: Trinity Rodman signs a new three-year deal with Washington Spirit that will reportedly make her the world's highest-paid female footballer.

items.title: Poker player’s punt on Wednesday shrouded in secrecy after Blades missteps
items.summary: James Bord’s consortium is the preferred bidder to take over Sheffield Wednesday but his data-led player recruitment record is mixed, especially with United Sheffield Wednesday fans will be delighted to hear that one associate of James Bord describes the preferred bidder for their club as “a mini Tony Bloom”, although the professional poker player’s references from the other side of the Steel City are rather less complimentary. Until it became clear late last year that Bord was planning to buy Wednesday his data company, Short Circuit Science, had a consultancy contract with Sheffield United to assist with their recruitment, which, as their position in the lower reaches of the Championship indicates, has delivered limited success. Continue reading...
